content.problemStatement:
For a positive, even\textbf{even}even integer NNN, we call a pair of arrays AAA and BBB to be **interesting** if they satisfy the following conditions :



âˆ£Aâˆ£=âˆ£Bâˆ£=N/2|A| = |B| = N/2âˆ£Aâˆ£=âˆ£Bâˆ£=N/2 i.e. the length of array AAA is equal to the length of array BBB.




Each integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly once in exactly one of the arrays.




The ithi^{th}ith prefix sum of AAA is not equal to ithi^{th}ith prefix sum of BBB for all 1â‰¤iâ‰¤N/2âˆ’11 \leq i \leq N/2-11â‰¤iâ‰¤N/2âˆ’1. 

Formally, âˆ‘j=1iAj\displaystyle \sum_{j = 1}^{i} A_jj=1âˆ‘iâ€‹Ajâ€‹ != âˆ‘j=1iBj \displaystyle \sum_{j = 1}^{i} B_jj=1âˆ‘iâ€‹Bjâ€‹ for all 1â‰¤iâ‰¤N/2âˆ’11 \leq i \leq N/2 - 11â‰¤iâ‰¤N/2âˆ’1




Sum of all elements in AAA is equal to sum of all elements in BBB i.e. âˆ‘j=1N/2Aj=âˆ‘j=1N/2Bj\displaystyle \sum_{j = 1}^{N/2} A_j = \sum_{j = 1}^{N/2} B_jj=1âˆ‘N/2â€‹Ajâ€‹=j=1âˆ‘N/2â€‹Bjâ€‹



You are given a positive, even integer NNN. If there exists an **interesting** pair of arrays, then print "YES" followed by an interesting pair for this given NNN. If there exists multiple **interesting** pairs of arrays for given NNN, you can print any. Print "NO" in a single line if no such pair exists.


Input Format

First line of input will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, the integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, if there exists an **interesting** pair of arrays, say (A,B)(A, B)(A,B), then in the first line print "YES", in the second line print array AAA separated by spaces, and in third line print array BBB separated by spaces. Print "NO" in a single line if no such pair exists. If there are multiple answers, print any of them.
constraints: NNN is guaranteed to be even.
Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 10610^6106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 2: Consider A=[1,4]A = [1, 4]A=[1,4] and B=[2,3]B = [2, 3]B=[2,3]. Every integer from 111 to 444 occurs exactly once in exactly one of the arrays. Also, 111st prefix sum of AAA is not equal to 111st prefix sum of BBB (1â‰ 21 \neq 21î€ =2). And sum of the elements is equal to 555 for both arrays. So, (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) is an interesting pair.
